What began on Aug. 1 is nearing its completion, as the track and field segment of the2024 Olympic Gamesin Paris is set for its final full day of action at Stade de France – and the nearby neighborhoods for the marathon.

The track and field portion of the games officially will end with the women’s marathon Sunday, which will conclude just a few hours before a festive closing ceremony celebrates more than two weeks of camaraderie and competition and serves as a sendoff for the more than 10,000 athletes who made Paris their home this summer.

Before the last race and emotional goodbye, however, is an action-packed Saturday that will feature nine event finals and nine more chances for athletes to reach the podium in Paris.

Saturday’s schedule will kick off at 1 a.m. Eastern with the men’s marathon and Eliud Kipchoge’s quest to become the first athlete to win the event three times. Oh, and at 39, he’s also the two-time defending Olympic champion.

Beyond that storyline is the fact that the marathon is one of the original events of the modern Olympics, which first were held in 1896.

Read more:When Are The Marathons At The 2024 Paris Olympic Games: What To Know

Medals also will be awarded in the men’s high jump, men’s 800, women’s javelin, women’s 100-meter hurdles, men’s 5,000, women’s 1,500 and the prestigious 4x400 relays.

Track and field fans already saw history made in the preliminary round of the men’s 4x400 on Friday when16-year-old Quincy Wilson ran the opening leg for the United States, making him the youngest Olympian in Team USA history.

Wilson helped the men qualify for Saturday’s final, and the women made the cut, too. That means a shot at more history, as the men seek their third consecutive gold medal in the event, and the women try to make it eight straight. That’s right, they’ve won every time since Atlanta in 1996!

Read more:When Are The 4x400 Relay Finals At The 2024 Olympics: What To Know

Will the final of the women’s 1,500 be as dramatic and exciting asTeam USA’s Cole Hocker upsetting favorites Jakob Ingebrigtsen and Josh Kerr in the men’s race?

Will the women’s 100-meter hurdles end with the same history and emotion asSydney McLaughlin-Levrone’s second Olympic win in the 400-meter hurdles?

Make sure to tune in to find out!

Here’s the schedule for Day 10 of the track and field competition at the 2024 Summer Olympics:

All Times Eastern

Teamwork makes the dream work, right? Well, the Team USA men got it done in Paris.

Team USA gets through 🇺🇸It was a close call for the auto qualifying spots, but the U.S. men take the third Q from heat one of the men's 4x400m first round in 2:59.15.Splits:1 - Quincy Wilson - 47.272 - Vernon Norwood - 43.543 - Bryce Deadmon - 44.204 - Chris Bailey -…pic.twitter.com/uGM1EjMypt

The Olympics are on the NBC family of networks, including the Peacock streaming app.
